title: Improve bot team coordination and strategic intelligence

requirements.text: Enhance bot AI teamwork for the Colonizer faction in `frontend/src/utils/botAI.ts`: Colonizer bots should coordinate as a team by dividing roles — Engineer bots prioritize building near the Fort, Captain bots position themselves near clustered allies to maximize their buff aura, Musket Soldier bots focus on contesting open-field strategic points, and Missionary bots stay close to damaged allies to heal rather than engage enemies directly. Colonizer bots should communicate intent implicitly through game state: if one bot is already capturing a strategic point, other Colonizer bots should move to support or cover a different uncaptured point rather than converging on the same one.

quotes: quero que os bots das equipes sejam  mais inteligentes e trabalham em equipe

acceptanceCriteria: Colonizer bots spread across all 3 strategic points rather than clustering on one
Engineer bots prioritize the Build action when adjacent to Fort territory instead of attacking
Captain bots reposition toward clusters of allied units to activate their buff aura
Missionary bots target wounded allies with healing actions before engaging enemies
Musket Soldier bots prefer open-field territories and exploit their range bonus there

requirements.text: Enhance bot AI teamwork for the Native Peoples faction in `frontend/src/utils/botAI.ts`: Native bots should coordinate defensively as a team — Forest Warrior bots use camouflage and ambush tactics by positioning in forest territories and waiting for Colonizer units to enter range before attacking, Spirit Hunter bots mark high-priority enemy targets (e.g., Engineers near the Fort) so that melee allies are guided toward the same target, Shaman bots stay in the rear and prioritize healing the most critically wounded ally each turn, and Sentinel bots actively seek and destroy enemy constructions (Towers, Fortresses) before taking offensive actions. Multiple Native bots should converge on the same contested territory when its capture countdown is active.

quotes: quero que os bots das equipes sejam  mais inteligentes e trabalham em equipe

acceptanceCriteria: Forest Warrior bots position in forest territories and wait for enemies rather than charging into open fields
Spirit Hunter bots use the enemy-marking ability on Engineer or high-value Colonizer targets
Shaman bots select the lowest-health allied unit as healing target each turn
Sentinel bots prioritize the Destroy action on any visible enemy construction over offensive moves
When a territory's capture progress bar is active, 2 or more Native bots converge on that territory to interrupt the capture

requirements.text: Implement a shared bot team state object in `frontend/src/utils/botAI.ts` that is computed once per team turn before individual bot decisions are made. This state should track: which strategic points are currently contested or uncaptured, which allied units are critically low on health (below 30%), which enemy constructions are visible, and which bot units have already been assigned a role this turn (capture, defend, heal, build/destroy). Each bot unit should read this shared state when selecting its action to avoid redundant targeting and improve coordination.

quotes: quero que os bots das equipes sejam  mais inteligentes e trabalham em equipe

acceptanceCriteria: A shared team context object is computed at the start of each bot team's turn before any individual unit acts
No two bot units on the same team are assigned the identical capture-point target unless all other points are covered
Bot units flagged as 'healer role' (Shaman, Missionary) do not attempt to capture territories when wounded allies are within movement range
Bot decision logs (console or comment-traceable) reflect role assignments from the shared state

constraints: Do not modify any files listed under frontend.immutablePaths
All bot AI changes must be confined to frontend/src/utils/botAI.ts and any helper utilities it imports; do not alter useGameEngine.ts game loop timing or win condition logic
nonGoals: Changes to human player controls or UI
Rebalancing unit base stats or territory capture timings
Adding new unit classes or abilities
